bbc.title: Aftershock frays nerves as many Venezuelans left to fend for themselves
bbc.description: In areas devastated by the twin earthquakes, people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.

bbc.title: 'No one move!': The agonising silence as Venezuela rescuers listen for survivors
bbc.description: With tens of thousands of people still believed missing, teams listen for signs of life beneath the rubble.

bbc.title: Six people shot dead at centre for mothers and children in Germany
bbc.description: The male suspect who has been arrested was in a custody dispute over his baby daughter, police say.

bbc.title: Trump's final appeal of E Jean Carroll sex abuse case rejected
bbc.description: The Supreme Court decision means the US president will now have to pay Carroll the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.

bbc.title: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
bbc.description: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

bbc.title: Pakistani strikes kill at least 28 civilians in Afghanistan, UN says
bbc.description: Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".

bbc.title: Putin makes rare admission of fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian strikes
bbc.description: The Russian president acknowledged Ukraine's attacks were "obviously creating problems" but denied the shortages were "critical".

bbc.title: South African leader warns anti-migrant protesters ahead of unofficial deadline
bbc.description: Thousands of people from other African countries have left South Africa ahead of Tuesday's deadline set by anti-migrant groups.

bbc.title: Homes harder to sell as high mortgage rates frustrate buyers
bbc.description: Three in five homes listed for sale since January remain on the market, says property portal Zoopla.

bbc.title: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook

bbc.title: British American Tobacco to cut 9,000 jobs

bbc.title: Ford rehires human engineers after AI fails to match quality checks

bbc.title: South Korea unveils $880bn chip and AI investment plan
